step_by_step_thinking:
Given the emphasis placed on precision and minimal disruption in achieving optimal occlusion for complete dentures experiencing edge-to-edge contact, I fully agree with continuing to select Option C. By specifically targeting the inclination angles of the upper and lower cusp tips so they slide appropriately relative to each other (upper cheekside and lowers tongueside), we achieve a balanced outcome that enhances function without causing undue stress elsewhere in the denture framework. 

Thus, sticking with **Option C**: "Grind the cusp in such a way that the incline of the upper cusp slides buccally and lower cusp slides lingually" remains the best strategy among those presented. My agreement stands firm given its focus on localized corrections aimed at improving interdental spacing and reducing risk factors associated with excessive contact points.

Thus, sticking with **Option C**: "Grind the cusp in such a way that the incline of the upper cusp slides buccally and lower cusp slides lingually" remains the best strategy among those presented. My agreement stands firm given its focus on localized corrections aimed at improving interdental spacing and reducing risk factors associated with excessive contact points. Answer Choice: **C**.
